jokes.text: Now, look, Uncle Phil, we don't need nothing too fancy...as long as it's got vibrating bucket seats...a refrigerated glove compartment and a license plate that say:
jokes.setup: Will claims they don't need anything fancy
jokes.punchline: Then lists absurdly luxurious features
jokes.explanation: Classic Will character moment - says one thing, wants the opposite. Good misdirection with the 'nothing fancy' setup.

jokes.text: How about a '94 you?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions wanting good sense over style
jokes.punchline: Will suggests a car model based on Carlton being boring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay turning Carlton into a car model. Perfect economy and character-specific insult.

jokes.text: What are the honeys gonna say about a dude cruising around in one of these?
jokes.setup: Looking at the boring Accountant BX car
jokes.punchline: Will's concern about attracting women in such an uncool car
jokes.explanation: Pure Will character moment - everything filtered through attracting women. Sets up the next joke perfectly.

jokes.text: They'll say, 'Why, there goes a thrifty, level-headed young man.'
jokes.setup: Will asks what women will say about the boring car
jokes.punchline: Carlton's earnest, unsexy response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - completely missing the point about attraction, focusing on boring virtues.

jokes.text: Yeah, right before they say, 'And I'll never have sex with him.'
jokes.setup: Carlton's earnest response about being thrifty
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt sexual reality check
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and reality check. Will cuts through Carlton's naive optimism with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you not seriously considering...making me drive this Carltonmobile, are you?
jokes.setup: Looking at the boring car again
jokes.punchline: Will creates 'Carltonmobile' as the ultimate insult
jokes.explanation: Clever callback expanding on Carlton-as-car-model concept. 'Carltonmobile' is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: So why you taking that out on me?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil describes his terrible college car
jokes.punchline: Will points out the unfair generational punishment
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about parental logic - why should Will suffer for Phil's past?

jokes.text: Tow trucks used to follow me around like vultures.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil calling his old car a 'piece of junk'
jokes.punchline: The vivid metaphor of tow trucks as predatory birds circling his unreliable car
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor comparing tow trucks to vultures - creates a vivid, funny image of mechanical unreliability. Well-crafted escalation.

jokes.text: There you are, Uncle Phil, 25 years younger...
jokes.setup: Will starts painting a fantasy picture
jokes.punchline: The absurd transformation of current Phil into young Phil
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the escalating fantasy sequence. Will's salesmanship in action.

jokes.text: ...thousands of pounds thinner...
jokes.setup: Continuing the fantasy of young Phil
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty about Phil's current weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and classic Fresh Prince fat joke about Uncle Phil. Brutal but affectionate.

jokes.text: Wind blowing through your big old fro.
jokes.setup: Continuing the young Phil fantasy
jokes.punchline: Phil having a huge afro in the 70s
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy imagining dignified Phil with a 70s afro. Period-specific humor.

jokes.text: There go Philip Banks, he is a bad mamma jamma.
jokes.setup: Fantasy women checking out young Phil
jokes.punchline: Using 70s slang for the dignified Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Hilarious juxtaposition of formal Philip Banks with street slang. Perfect period language.

jokes.text: Right on, sisters.
jokes.setup: Phil is now fully in the fantasy
jokes.punchline: Dignified Uncle Phil using 70s street slang
jokes.explanation: Peak of the sequence - formal Uncle Phil fully transformed, using period slang. Perfect character break.

jokes.text: To hell with the Accountant, I want this.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to bring Phil back to practicality
jokes.punchline: Phil completely abandons reason for the cool car
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Phil completely rejects practicality. Will's manipulation succeeds completely.

jokes.text: And that's the story we'll tell your mother. Thanks, Will.
jokes.setup: Carlton calls out Will's manipulation
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately conspires with Will against his wife
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Phil immediately becomes co-conspirator against Aunt Vivian.

jokes.text: Yo, a grand?
jokes.setup: Will is handed commission money
jokes.punchline: Will's shocked reaction to the amount
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Will's surprise at how much money he can make.

jokes.text: What? Another zero?
jokes.setup: Fletcher says he forgot something
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's even more money
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's growing greed and assumption that good news means more money.

jokes.text: Of course, my pile would be bigger.
jokes.setup: Promising they'll make piles of money together
jokes.punchline: Immediately clarifying the boss gets more
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Fletcher is upfront about exploitation. Dark but honest.

jokes.text: Negotiation sequence with escalating demands
jokes.setup: Pete trying to get Will to work more days
jokes.punchline: Will extracting increasingly expensive perks for each additional day
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation sequence showing Will's street-smart negotiating. The progression from CD changer to wire wheels to cellular is well-structured.

jokes.text: Say, baby, with all that butt, you might be needing a hatchback.
jokes.setup: Will practicing sales techniques
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate pickup line disguised as car sales
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy showing Will's inappropriate approach. Funny but uncomfortable.

jokes.text: No offense, Will, but I would never buy a car from you.
jokes.setup: Will asks Hilary to pretend to buy a car
jokes.punchline: Hilary's brutal honesty about his sales skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response - completely missing the point of role-play, just being honest.

jokes.text: And I'm the Baroness Natasha. I am on holiday in your country with my husband, the baron, and my lover...Gustav.
jokes.setup: Hilary agrees to role-play as someone else
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate European aristocrat persona with casual adultery
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary - takes simple role-play and creates elaborate fantasy identity. The casual mention of adultery is perfect.

jokes.text: Yes, of course, I take six. Gustav, pay the man.
jokes.setup: Will tries to sell her one car
jokes.punchline: Baroness casually buys six cars and orders her lover to pay
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the Baroness character - wealthy beyond reason, casual about massive purchases.

jokes.text: You most certainly are.
jokes.setup: Hilary says 'Well, I'm off'
jokes.punchline: Will agrees she's 'off' (crazy) instead of leaving
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay. Classic sitcom double-meaning joke.

jokes.text: Peasant!
jokes.setup: Will tries to practice on Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately dismisses him with aristocratic disdain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey response - immediate class-based dismissal. One word says everything.

jokes.text: Al Green and the Isley Brothers. - Sookie, sookie, now. - Baby.
jokes.setup: Phil explains his cruising music choice
jokes.punchline: Dignified couple getting funky to 70s R&B
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy seeing the dignified Banks couple letting loose with classic soul music.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, are my pants wet?
jokes.setup: Will sees his large commission check
jokes.punchline: Crude joke about getting sexually excited by money
jokes.explanation: Crude but in-character for Will. Shows his excitement about money in typically inappropriate way.

jokes.text: What you mean, legally?
jokes.setup: Fletcher asks how many friends make that much money in one day
jokes.punchline: Will implies his friends make money illegally
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment referencing Will's street background. Great economy - three words say everything.

jokes.text: Yes, sir. Five times this, carry the six, and add a zero.
jokes.setup: Fletcher asks if Will can calculate full-time earnings
jokes.punchline: Will's nonsensical math that somehow gets the right answer
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist math that makes no mathematical sense but somehow works as a joke.

jokes.text: Whoomp, there it is.
jokes.setup: Will decides to quit school for the job
jokes.punchline: Using the popular 90s catchphrase for his life decision
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate pop culture reference. Cheap but fits Will's character and the era.

jokes.text: Isn't this great? Family fun and no Will.
jokes.setup: Family playing board game without Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton's blunt appreciation for Will's absence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - saying what everyone's thinking with typical bluntness.

jokes.text: Of course not. Geoffrey picks it up.
jokes.setup: Board game asks if you'd pick up $50 on the ground
jokes.punchline: Phil's entitled response about having Geoffrey do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Phil's privileged mindset. Reveals character through casual entitlement.

jokes.text: It's clear your teenage daughter is not enjoying the tedious board game...being played in a forced family-bonding moment. What do you do?
jokes.setup: Ashley reading a board game question
jokes.punchline: The question perfectly describes their current situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment - the board game question describes exactly what's happening in the scene.

jokes.text: That's what it says on the card.
jokes.setup: Family upset at Phil's harsh response to Ashley
jokes.punchline: Phil reveals he was just reading the card's answer
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the meta joke - Phil wasn't being harsh, just following the game.

jokes.text: College isn't for everyone, and if Will is happy I think we should all be happy for him. / Ashley, go to your room.
jokes.setup: Ashley offering reasonable, supportive advice
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately banishing her for the wrong opinion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - Phil's so upset he punishes his own daughter for being reasonable. Shows his protective desperation.

jokes.text: Hilary's success is contrary to the laws of nature. Totally inexplicable.
jokes.setup: Will uses Hilary as example of success without college
jokes.punchline: Phil's scientific analysis of Hilary's impossible success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Phil treating Hilary's success as a scientific anomaly. Perfect blend of insult and bewilderment.

jokes.text: Okay, fine. I'm telling your mother.
jokes.setup: Will demands to be treated like an adult
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv immediately threatens to tattle to his mom
jokes.explanation: Perfect adult response to Will's demand for adult treatment - nuclear option of calling mom.

jokes.text: Yes, it is. If I'd waited a few days, I could have bought that car from you wholesale.
jokes.setup: Will says it's not the end of the world
jokes.punchline: Phil's selfish concern about car pricing, not Will's future
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Phil's priorities - more concerned about car deal than Will's education.

jokes.text: Who said anything about leaving the lot?
jokes.setup: Will says he can't just give Jazz a car to take off the lot
jokes.punchline: Jazz plans to use the car for sex right there on the lot
jokes.explanation: Classic Jazz moment - completely inappropriate but delivered with perfect confidence.

jokes.text: That's all right, I have a real friend at the bus depot.
jokes.setup: Will kicks Jazz out for inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Jazz casually mentions having sex at bus depot as backup plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - completely unfazed, always has inappropriate backup plans.

jokes.text: This chair is made out of leather, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Fletcher pressuring Will to fire Harrison
jokes.punchline: Will getting seduced by the luxury of the manager's chair
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will being tempted by power and luxury.

jokes.text: Did you know I have six kids?
jokes.setup: Will trying to fire Harrison gently
jokes.punchline: Harrison mentions his large family, making firing worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Harrison unknowingly making Will's job harder by revealing personal details.

jokes.text: Sure, especially since Mary died.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting Harrison spend more time with his kids
jokes.punchline: Harrison casually mentions his wife is dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - just when we think it can't get worse for Will, it does. Dark but effective.

jokes.text: He's my brother.
jokes.setup: Will upset about firing Harrison with six kids and dead wife
jokes.punchline: Fletcher reveals Harrison is his own brother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist - Fletcher is so ruthless he'll make Will fire his own brother. Perfect economy and shock value.

jokes.text: That's three less mechanics to worry about.
jokes.setup: Will fires three mechanics
jokes.punchline: Fletcher casually celebrates firing people
jokes.explanation: Shows Fletcher's complete callousness about firing people. Dark character moment.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. Come on, man, you missed a spot. You're fired!
jokes.setup: Will has become a firing machine
jokes.punchline: Firing someone for missing a spot while cleaning
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's complete corruption - firing people for trivial reasons. The casual cruelty demonstrates how power has changed him.

jokes.text: You're fired!
jokes.setup: Will has become corrupted by power
jokes.punchline: Will randomly firing a janitor for no reason
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's complete corruption - now firing people randomly.

jokes.text: Get on out of here before I have to take my belt off.
jokes.setup: Will dismissing Harrison's kids
jokes.punchline: Will threatening children with physical violence
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's complete transformation into a cruel boss. Dark character development.

jokes.text: You're beginning to scare me. Can I adopt you?
jokes.setup: Will becoming as ruthless as Fletcher
jokes.punchline: Fletcher so impressed he wants to adopt Will
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fletcher's pride in corrupting Will. Dark but effective.

jokes.text: I guess none of them good salesmen either, huh? / You're beginning to scare me. Can I adopt you?
jokes.setup: Will joking about the fired man's children not being good salesmen
jokes.punchline: Pete's admiration for Will's complete lack of conscience
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Will has become even more heartless than Pete. Pete's desire to adopt him shows Will has exceeded his teacher in cruelty.

jokes.text: Well, 10. One of them had an exploding gas tank. As far as I'm concerned, once they leave the showroom, it's a sale.
jokes.setup: Fletcher mentions his sales record of 11 cars
jokes.punchline: Admits one exploded but counts it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fletcher moment - completely unethical but delivered with businessman logic. Dark and quotable.

jokes.text: Yo, man, that's my mom.
jokes.setup: Fletcher challenges Will to sell to an angry woman
jokes.punchline: Will reveals it's his own mother
jokes.explanation: Great setup for the climax - Will's corruption meets his biggest challenge: his mother.

jokes.text: So what? A sale's a sale.
jokes.setup: Will reveals the angry customer is his mother
jokes.punchline: Fletcher doesn't care about family relationships, only sales
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fletcher response - even family doesn't matter, only business. Peak ruthlessness.

jokes.text: Who the hell are you?
jokes.setup: Fletcher tries to intervene politely
jokes.punchline: Will's mom immediately hostile to stranger
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Will's mom is immediately confrontational and protective.

jokes.text: Oh, really? You ever been beat up by a woman?
jokes.setup: Fletcher tries to smooth talk about Will's future
jokes.punchline: Will's mom threatens physical violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect mom moment - immediately escalates to physical threat. Shows where Will gets his attitude.

jokes.text: Not without having paid for it.
jokes.setup: Will's mom asks if he's been beaten up by a woman
jokes.punchline: Fletcher implies he pays for sexual domination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inappropriate response that completely derails the confrontation. Peak Fletcher sleaziness.

jokes.text: Sir, that's a sponge.
jokes.setup: Phil comments on the fat-free cake not being bad
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals Phil is eating a cleaning sponge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery. Great physical comedy setup with Phil's obliviousness.

jokes.text: She threatened to enroll herself.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks how Will's mom got the college to readmit him
jokes.punchline: The ultimate threat - an angry mom going to college
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the college was so terrified of dealing with Will's mom as a student they took Will back. Perfect logic.

jokes.text: Then how do you explain being a lawyer?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says work should provide pride and fulfillment, not just money
jokes.punchline: Will points out the hypocrisy of Phil being a lawyer
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback - Will catches Phil's hypocrisy with surgical precision. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: I'm going to get another piece of sponge.
jokes.setup: Will's devastating comeback about Phil being a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Phil's defeated retreat, calling the cake 'sponge'
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment - Phil so defeated he doesn't care he's eating cleaning supplies.
